symptoms.symptom_name: New profound confusion / altered conscious state over 12 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Metabolic encephalopathy is a hallmark of severe uraemia.
Confusion is non-specific; global metabolic pattern without focal deficit argues against stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intractable nausea and vomiting refractory to antiemetics

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uraemia drives intractable vomiting; failure of antiemetics points to a metabolic cause needing dialysis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urine-like odour on the breath (uraemic fetor)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uraemic fetor is relatively specific for advanced uraemia and helps distinguish from fetor hepaticus.
A urine-like odour argues against the sweet/musty fetor hepaticus of liver failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Massive peripheral oedema with exertional dyspnoea (volume overload)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Salt-and-water retention from absent kidney function; breathlessness signals pulmonary oedema.
No urine output despite diuretics confirms loss of effective renal function and predicts hyperkalaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised pruritus with excoriation, anorexia and weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uraemic pruritus and anorexia/cachexia are features of advanced chronic kidney failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Caregiver-reported severe hypertension and tachycardia (BP 195/110, HR 105) and hypoxia (SpO2 92%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Volume-dependent hypertension and hypoxia from pulmonary oedema; caregiver-reported and unverified in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Asterixis (bilateral flapping tremor on wrist extension)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asterixis indicates metabolic encephalopathy and, with a global picture, argues against focal stroke — a sealed clinician bedside finding the AI cannot elicit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated jugular venous pressure and bilateral coarse crackles (pulmonary oedema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician signs of fluid overload — sealed; the AI infers overload from reported swelling and breathlessness, not from auscultation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Estimated glomerular filtration rate persistently <15 mL/min/1.73 m2

investigation_details.expected_result: eGFR persistently below 15 mL/min/1.73 m2 (established ESRD)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of renal failure and dialysis need cannot be confirmed without bloods.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Persistent eGFR <15 defines ESRD — a laboratory value unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Potentially critically elevated in anuric ESRD — not measured in this encounter
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Undetected life-threatening hyperkalaemia can cause sudden fatal arrhythmia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cannot be measured or excluded via telehealth; its risk is a primary driver of emergency escalation.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute uraemic crisis in end-stage renal disease
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Encephalopathy + intractable vomiting + uraemic fetor + anuria together indicate severe uraemia needing emergency dialysis.
Massive oedema, hypoxia and clinician-detected crackles/raised JVP signal fluid overload only dialysis can offload.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency serum electrolytes (especially potassium), urea, creatinine/eGFR and venous blood gas — in hospital
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms severity of uraemia and detects life-threatening hyperkalaemia; obtained after emergency transfer, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG for hyperkalaemic changes — in hospital
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for peaked T waves / arrhythmia risk before and during dialysis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest imaging for pulmonary oedema — in hospital
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies volume overload; not required to trigger the emergency, which rests on the clinical picture.
The encephalopathy is metabolic (asterixis, global, no focal deficit); routing primarily to a stroke pathway would delay the correct dialysis pathway — reassess only if focal signs emerge.
